description:
A single account is producing multiple gauge series and multiple time-until-reset values in the dashboard. This is likely caused by extra labels beyond account and org_id (e.g. job, instance, service_name, or other OTEL resource attributes promoted to Prometheus labels) causing the metric to fan out into multiple time series.

Fix approach:
1. Identify the extra labels by running a raw query like claude_usage_weekly_utilization_percent in Prometheus and inspecting the full label set
2. In the dashboard PromQL queries, aggregate away the extra labels using sum by (account, org_id) or max by (account, org_id) on all panel queries — whichever is semantically correct (max for utilization, min for reset timestamps)
3. Apply consistently to all affected panels: utilization gauges, reset countdowns, time series panels

The root cause may also be in the exporter itself if it is emitting duplicate measurements — check poller.go to confirm each account only records one observation per poll cycle.
notes: Re-implemented after MR rejection: now aggregates by (account, org_id) instead of just (account), and covers all 4 files including grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json and grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json which were missed in first attempt. Total: 33 PromQL expressions wrapped with max/min/sum by (account, org_id).
title: Fix duplicate chart series for single account

This lets Grafana assign palette colours naturally, matching the actual series.

description:
attached_molecule: cue-wisp-k9vya
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-03T20:35:23Z
dispatched_by: mayor

When there is no active session, the ideal usage lines (Ideal — session, Ideal — weekly) continue to show the last value rather than showing nothing.

## Root cause (updated)

The spanNulls: false fix in the dashboard overrides was wrong. spanNulls only creates visual gaps when the query returns actual nulls — but the ideal line queries return continuous values regardless of session state (they are calculated from configured limits, not session data). So the series never produces null and spanNulls has no effect.

## Correct fix

The PromQL/VictoriaMetrics query for both ideal lines needs to be gated on session activity. Multiply the ideal line value by a boolean expression that is 1 when a session is active and absent/0 when no session is active — so the metric itself produces no data outside active sessions.

For example:
  ideal_session_value * on() group_left() (clamp_min(session_active_metric, 1))

or use `and` / `unless` vector matching to suppress the series when no session reset timestamp is being emitted.

Identify the exact metric used for session liveness and update both ideal line queries in the dashboard accordingly. Also remove or correct the spanNulls: false overrides if they are now redundant.
title: Ideal lines should not plot when there is no active session

description:
The Nix-built Docker image can be made much smaller. Key optimisations to apply in flake.nix:

1. Strip Go binary debug symbols and symbol table by adding `ldflags = [ "-s" "-w" ];` to the `buildGoModule` attrs. This is the biggest win.
2. Set `CGO_ENABLED = "0";` in the `buildGoModule` attrs for a fully static binary (no libc dependency).
3. Review whether all cacert content is needed — pkgs.cacert includes the full Mozilla bundle; consider pkgs.cacert.unbundled or just confirming we need the full bundle for claude.ai TLS.
4. Consider using `pkgs.dockerTools.buildImage` instead of `buildLayeredImage` if layers aren't needed (simpler, often smaller for single-binary images). Either way is fine.

The binary is currently unstripped Go which can be 10-20MB. With -s -w stripping it should drop to ~5-8MB. The overall image should be well under 20MB.

Verify by running `docker images` before and after to compare sizes.
No behaviour changes — only build flags and image config.
notes: Implemented: Added env.CGO_ENABLED=0 and ldflags=[-s -w] to buildGoModule in flake.nix. Docker image reduced from 18MB to 6.5MB (64% reduction). Binary reduced from 17MB to 15MB. Kept full cacert bundle (needed for claude.ai TLS) and buildLayeredImage. No behaviour changes.
title: Minimise container image size

attached_at: 2026-03-03T17:12:09Z
dispatched_by: mayor

The account variable has hide:2 (invisible) but skipUrlSync is not set. If the browser URL contains var-account=All from a previous session, Grafana overrides the variable to "All" even though includeAll:false — causing the repeat panel to render as "Utilization Over Time — All" with no data.

Fix: add "skipUrlSync": true to the account variable in grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json. This makes the variable always use its dashboard-JSON default rather than URL state.
title: Set skipUrlSync: true on account variable to prevent URL state override

description:
## Problem

There are 3 dashboard JSON files, only one of which Grafana actually reads:

| File | Status | Panels | UID |
|------|--------|--------|-----|
| grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json | **LIVE** — provisioned via Docker volume mount | 11 | claude-usage |
| dashboards/claude-usage.json | Dead — original pre-Docker-Compose file (importable format) | 7 | (none) |
| grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json | Dead — importable format with __inputs datasource picker | 7 | claude-usage-exporter |

Polecats have been inconsistently updating the wrong files (e.g. cue-m64 wrote to the wrong file).

## What to do

1. Read all 3 files and compare panel-by-panel
2. For each panel in the dead files not already covered by a panel in the live file:
   - If the query/logic is genuinely different/better, add it to grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json
   - If it's just a rename of something already present, skip it
3. Delete dashboards/claude-usage.json and grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json
4. Commit and push

## Known unique panels in dead files (verify before deciding)

**dashboards/claude-usage.json** has: Adaptive Poll Interval, Time Until Session Reset, Time Until Weekly Reset, Session Utilization, Weekly Utilization — these look like older names for panels already in the provisioned file. Confirm by comparing queries.

**grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json** has: Poll Interval (Adaptive), CC OTEL Correlation: Usage Exporter ↔ Claude Code Host Metrics (different title from provisioned), Session/Weekly Utilization by Org (CC OTEL Join Key) — again likely renamed duplicates. The CC OTEL table has a subtly different query structure (check the label_replace logic).

## Acceptance

- Only grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json remains
- dashboards/ directory and grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json are deleted
- All genuinely unique query logic from the dead files is preserved or documented as intentionally dropped
- Grafana provisioning still works (docker-compose up loads the dashboard correctly)
notes: Analysis: All panels in both dead files are duplicates of panels in the live provisioned dashboard. Dead file 1 (dashboards/claude-usage.json) has 7 panels all covered by live. Dead file 2 (grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json) has 7 panels all covered by live, plus uses wrong metric name (poll_interval_s vs poll_interval_seconds). Neither has unique query logic worth preserving. Deleting both dead files and the empty dashboards/ directory.
title: Consolidate Grafana dashboards: merge dead files into provisioned dashboard

description:
The Grafana dashboard in dashboards/claude-usage.json references metric names that don't match what the exporter actually produces. The dashboard shows no data as a result.

Rename all references in the dashboard JSON:

| Dashboard references             | Actual metric name                       |
|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| claude_usage_session_utilization | claude_usage_session_utilization_percent |
| claude_usage_weekly_utilization  | claude_usage_weekly_utilization_percent  |
| claude_usage_session_reset       | claude_usage_session_reset_seconds       |
| claude_usage_weekly_reset        | claude_usage_weekly_reset_seconds        |
| claude_usage_poll_interval       | claude_usage_poll_interval_seconds       |

claude_usage_poll_errors_total is correct — do not change it.

Also update the README metrics table to use the correct suffixed names if it hasn't been already.

The fix is a find-and-replace in the dashboard JSON. Verify by checking that no old names remain after the change.
notes: Implemented: Renamed all 5 incorrect metric names in both dashboard JSON files (dashboards/claude-usage.json and grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json) and updated the README metrics table with correct Prometheus-suffixed names (_percent, _seconds). Verified poll_errors_total was not changed. JSON validated.
title: Fix dashboard metric names to match exporter output

description:
Add a validation script (scripts/lint-dashboards.js or similar) that checks grafana/dashboards/*.json before merge. Should fail on:

1. Variables with type="query" where `query` is a plain string (old format) — must be object `{qryType: 1, query: "..."}` for Grafana 10+
2. Repeat panels referencing a variable name not present in the dashboard templating list
3. Optionally: datasource UIDs that differ from the known provisioned UID

Wire it into:
- `make check` (or a new `make lint-dashboards` target)
- CI (add a step to the existing workflow)

Reference: the bug where the account variable used the old string format meant it silently never populated in newer Grafana.
title: Add dashboard JSON linting to catch Grafana compatibility issues

description:
attached_molecule: cue-wisp-kdk7d
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-02-28T09:03:32Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Create .beads/formulas/claude-usage-exporter-release.formula.toml adapted from my_round's release formula. Steps:

1. verify-orientation — check pwd, git remote -v points to GraemeF/claude-usage-exporter, correct repo
2. preflight-git — git status clean
3. preflight-pull — git pull --rebase origin main
4. review-changes — git log since last tag --oneline to understand what's being released
5. run-build — nix build .#default and nix build .#dockerImage to verify it compiles (no test suite yet — note this in step)
6. fix-failures — only if build failed, file beads and sling polecats
7. bump-version — update version string in flake.nix (version = "{{version}}"), commit with 'chore: Release v{{version}}'
8. create-tag — git tag -a v{{version}} -m 'Release v{{version}}'
9. push-main — git push origin main
10. push-tag — git push origin v{{version}} (triggers GHA docker build)
11. wait-ci — monitor GHA, expect ~5 min
12. create-gh-release — HANDWRITTEN notes required, DO NOT use --generate-notes. Use gh release create v{{version}} with sections: What's new, Fixes, Docker image pull command (ghcr.io/graemef/claude-usage-exporter:v{{version}}). Verify at GitHub releases page.
13. verify-image — docker pull ghcr.io/graemef/claude-usage-exporter:v{{version}}, confirm it starts
14. release-complete — summary

NO deploy-verify step (no auto-deployment). Formula var: version (required, semver e.g. 1.0.0).
notes: Implemented: Created .beads/formulas/claude-usage-exporter-release.formula.toml with 14 sequential steps: verify-orientation, preflight-git, preflight-pull, review-changes, run-build, fix-failures, bump-version, create-tag, push-main, push-tag, wait-ci, create-gh-release, verify-image, release-complete. Single required var: version (semver). Adapted from my-round-release formula. Key differences: nix build instead of make verify, flake.nix version bump instead of gleam.toml, no deploy-verify step, no test suite noted. Formula validated: parses correctly, all 14 steps with linear dependency chain.
title: Create release formula

description:
attached_molecule: cue-wisp-28kig
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-02-28T09:03:32Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Adapt from my_round workflows. Three files:

1. .github/workflows/docker.yml — reusable workflow_call, inputs: version. Install Nix (cachix/install-nix-action@v30), nix build .#dockerImage, docker load < result, tag and push to ghcr.io as :version and :latest. Use GITHUB_REPOSITORY lowercase for image name.

2. .github/workflows/test.yml — on push to main (paths-ignore .beads/**) and pull_request and workflow_call. Jobs: build (nix build .#default), flake-check (nix flake check). Alert-on-failure job if main branch fails: create GitHub issue with ci-failure label (same pattern as my_round).

3. .github/workflows/release.yml — on push to tags v*. concurrency group. Calls docker.yml with version: github.ref_name. NO deploy job. After docker job: create-release job using gh release create with --title and --notes (placeholder notes — the formula handles the real notes). Actually: the release workflow should NOT create the GH release — that is done manually via the formula with handwritten notes. So release.yml just calls docker.yml.

No gleam-specific steps. No deploy step.
notes: Implemented three GHA workflows adapted from my_round patterns: test.yml (build + flake-check + alert-on-failure), docker.yml (reusable Nix docker build + ghcr.io push), release.yml (v* tag trigger calling docker workflow). No gleam steps, no deploy job, no GH release creation.
title: Add GHA workflows (test, docker, release)

description:
The poll error rate panel uses rate(claude_usage_poll_errors_total[5m]) which produces ops/s — meaninglessly small fractions at these poll intervals (30s–5m).

Replace with increase(claude_usage_poll_errors_total[1h]) to show error count over the last hour, or increase() over a rolling window that makes sense for the poll cadence. A stat panel showing total errors in the last hour is more actionable than a rate in ops/s.

Update both the query and the panel unit accordingly (no unit, just a count).
notes: Implemented: replaced rate(claude_usage_poll_errors_total[5m]) with increase(claude_usage_poll_errors_total[1h]) across all 3 dashboard files and the alert rule. Updated panel units from ops to none (plain count). Updated panel titles to 'Poll Errors (1h)' and legend formats to remove 'errors/s'.

description:
The current increase(claude_usage_poll_errors_total[1h]) produces a ramp that accumulates over the hour window — it does not show when errors are occurring or whether there is currently a problem.

Replace with increase(claude_usage_poll_errors_total[5m]) across all dashboard files. This produces a time series that:
- Spikes when errors are happening
- Returns to zero when polling recovers
- Makes it immediately obvious if there is a current problem

Also update panel title to reflect the new window (e.g. "Poll Errors (5m)").

Apply to all 4 files: dashboards/claude-usage.json, grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json, grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json, and alerts/claude-usage.yaml if applicable.
notes: Implemented: Changed poll errors increase() window from [1h] to [5m] across all 4 dashboard/alert files. Updated panel titles to 'Poll Errors (5m)'. The 5m window produces spikes instead of ramps, making current errors immediately visible.
title: Fix poll errors chart to show spikes not ramp

description:
cue-07p removed the per-account repeat panel by mistake. Need to restore it — but also fix the root cause of why it appeared broken.

ROOT CAUSE: The `account` variable had `includeAll: true`, so Grafana offered an "All" option. When "All" was selected, the repeat panel rendered once as "Utilization Over Time — All" with query filter `{account=~"All"}` which matched nothing → "No data".

WHAT TO DO:
1. Restore the `account` template variable with `includeAll: false` and `multi: true` so Grafana repeats the panel once per actual account value (never a fake "All" panel).
2. Restore the `Utilization Over Time — $account` timeseries panel with `repeat: account` — one chart per account showing session utilization %, weekly utilization %, and their ideal lines.

Use `git show 2b52aed:grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json` as reference for the panel JSON, but change the variable to have `includeAll: false`.

Keep the existing "Cumulative Usage — All Accounts" panel.
title: Restore per-account Utilization Over Time charts (cue-07p removed wrong panel)

description:
Add a "refresh" field to dashboards/claude-usage.json so the dashboard polls automatically.

Set \"refresh\": \"30s\" to match the exporter's default activeInterval. Also set a sensible \"time\" default range (e.g. \"from\": \"now-1h\", \"to\": \"now\") if not already present.

The field goes at the top level of the dashboard JSON object, alongside \"title\", \"uid\", etc.
notes: Implemented: Added "refresh": "30s" to both dashboards/claude-usage.json and grafana/dashboards/claude-usage.json. Also tightened default time range from now-6h to now-1h to match the 30s polling cadence.
title: Add auto-refresh to Grafana dashboard

description:
attached_molecule: cue-wisp-5pnf8
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-02T09:42:29Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Add a new Grafana panel to the main dashboard (grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json) showing all accounts summed together in a single chart, so we can see whether we have the right total number of accounts.

## What to build

A time-series panel with **4 lines**:

1. **Actual session usage** — sum of session utilisation across all accounts
2. **Ideal session line** — what that sum should be if all accounts burn linearly through their 5-hour window
3. **Actual weekly usage** — sum of weekly utilisation across all accounts
4. **Ideal weekly line** — what that sum should be if all accounts burn linearly through their 7-day window

Y-axis: summed utilisation % (range 0–N×100 where N = number of accounts).
The "right number" signal: if the sum tracks the ideal line, capacity matches demand.

## Prometheus queries

Use the existing metrics. The per-account ideal line logic is already in the dashboard — adapt it for sum aggregation.

Actual session (sum across all accounts):
  sum(claude_usage_session_utilization_percent)

Actual weekly (sum across all accounts):
  sum(claude_usage_weekly_utilization_percent)

Ideal session sum — sum the per-account ideal value using the per-account reset time:
  sum(clamp_min(clamp_max((1 - (claude_usage_session_reset_seconds - time()) / 18000) * 100, 100), 0))

Ideal weekly sum:
  sum(clamp_min(clamp_max((1 - (claude_usage_weekly_reset_seconds - time()) / 604800) * 100, 100), 0))

## Reference

Existing per-account panel: "Utilisation Over Time — $account" in grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json
Copy its style (legend, axis config, thresholds) and adapt queries as above.

Visual style: session lines in one colour family (e.g. blue/light-blue), weekly in another (e.g. green/light-green). Ideal lines dashed.

Panel title: "Cumulative Usage — All Accounts"
Place it near the top of the dashboard, before the per-account repeat panels.

## Steps

1. Read grafana/claude-usage-exporter.json to understand panel structure
2. Copy an existing time-series panel as template
3. Replace queries with the four sum-based queries above
4. Set legend labels: "Session (actual)", "Session (ideal)", "Weekly (actual)", "Weekly (ideal)"
5. Add panel to dashboard JSON, commit and push to main

No Go code changes needed — this is dashboard JSON only.
title: Dashboard: cumulative chart combining all accounts for session and weekly limits

description:
attached_molecule: cue-wisp-yj788
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-02-28T15:59:58Z
dispatched_by: mayor

On the utilisation-over-time chart, overlay an ideal usage line that ramps linearly from 0% to 100% across the period (session or week).

The ideal line represents perfectly even spending — if you have a session budget or weekly budget, it counts up proportionally so you can see at a glance whether you're ahead or behind the ideal pace.

- For session view: line goes from 0% at session start to 100% at session end
- For week view: line goes from 0% at week start to 100% at week end
- Should be visually distinct from actual usage (e.g. dashed line, different colour)
- Actual usage above the ideal line = burning faster than ideal; below = under-utilising
notes: Implemented ideal utilisation lines on all three dashboard variants. Session ideal: linear ramp 0→100% over 5h window using session_reset_seconds. Weekly ideal: linear ramp 0→100% over 7d window using weekly_reset_seconds. Lines are white dashed to distinguish from actual usage. PromQL uses clamp_min/clamp_max for clean 0-100 bounds.
title: Plot ideal utilisation line on usage-over-time chart

description:
The provisioned dashboard uses the old string format for the variable query:
  "query": "label_values(claude_usage_session_utilization_percent, account)"

Newer Grafana requires the object format with qryType. Without it, the variable never populates, stays at $__all, and the repeat panel renders as "Utilization Over Time — All" with no data.

Fix: replace the variable query with the format from the working (now deleted) dashboards/claude-usage.json:
```json
"query": {
  "qryType": 1,
  "query": "label_values(claude_usage_session_utilization_percent, account)"
},
"definition": "label_values(claude_usage_session_utilization_percent, account)",
"options": []
```

Also keep: multi: true, includeAll: false, hide: 2, refresh: 2, sort: 1, current: {}
title: Fix account variable query format for newer Grafana (never worked in provisioned dashboard)

Add host.name as an OTEL resource attribute so every metric includes the hostname of the machine running the exporter. Use os.Hostname() by default, overridable via OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES or a top-level 'hostname' field in accounts.yaml. This lets operators correlate which host is consuming which account's quota in Prometheus/Grafana. The standard OTEL Prometheus exporter promotes resource attributes to metric labels.
title: Add host attribute to all metrics

close_reason: Merged to main via hq-wisp-ki8siv. Deep investigation complete, Grafana panel fixes applied.
description:
## Required outcome
The dashboard must show ONE timeseries panel per Claude account (currently 2: claude-2@codeforbreakfast.co and graeme@codeforbreakfast.co), each titled "Utilization Over Time — <account>", showing session %, weekly %, and their ideal lines over time. The `account` variable must remain hidden from the toolbar.

## What is happening instead
A single panel titled "Utilization Over Time — All" appears with No data.

## Datasource UID is NOT the issue
All other panels (cumulative timeseries, gauges, stat panels) use `"uid": "prometheus"` and show real data correctly. The problem is specific to the repeat panel / account variable interaction.

## The panel worked in the deleted importable dashboard
`dashboards/claude-usage.json` was removed by cue-9ty. That file had a working repeat panel. Structural differences vs the current provisioned panel:

1. **`editorMode: "code"`** on each target — present in working, absent in current provisioned panel
2. **`pluginVersion: "11.0.0"`** on the panel — present in working, absent in current
3. **`maxPerRow: 1`** on the panel — present in working, absent in current
4. **Variable `current: {}`** (empty) in working vs `current: {text:"All", value:"$__all"}` in current
5. **Variable had no `options: []` field** in working version
6. **Variable had no `hide` or `skipUrlSync`** in working version (those are fine to keep)

## Working variable JSON (from deleted dashboard)
```json
{
  "name": "account", "type": "query",
  "datasource": { "type": "prometheus", "uid": "prometheus" },
  "query": { "qryType": 1, "query": "label_values(claude_usage_session_utilization_percent, account)" },
  "definition": "label_values(claude_usage_session_utilization_percent, account)",
  "includeAll": true, "multi": true,
  "current": {},
  "refresh": 2, "sort": 1
}
```

## Hypothesis
With `current: {text:"All", value:"$__all"}`, Grafana uses "All" as the literal variable value for the repeat without expanding it. With `current: {}`, Grafana executes the query on load and selects the actual account values, so repeat creates one panel per account.

The `options: []` field may also be confusing Grafana into thinking the variable has no available values.

## Fix to try
1. Set `current: {}` (empty object, not the text/value form)
2. Remove `options: []` from the variable
3. Add `editorMode: "code"` to each target, `pluginVersion: "11.0.0"` and `maxPerRow: 1` to the panel
4. Keep `hide: 2`, `skipUrlSync: true`, `includeAll: true`, `multi: true`, `qryType:1` query format

Port the exact panel fieldConfig and options from the working deleted dashboard (full JSON in git at `git show f7939dc^:dashboards/claude-usage.json`).
notes:
Root cause: provisioned dashboards with current:{text:'All',value:'$__all'} and empty options:[] cause Grafana's processRepeats() to fail. When isAllVariable() is true, Grafana does options.slice(1) — but with options:[], this returns [] and zero repeat panels are created.

Fix applied:
1. Variable: current:{} (forces Grafana to resolve from query), removed options:[], changed refresh:2→1 (run on dashboard load, not just time range change)
2. Panel: added pluginVersion:'11.0.0', full custom fieldConfig from working dashboard, editorMode:'code' and explicit datasource on each target, showLegend:true, threshold 60→50 matching working version
title: Deep investigation: Utilization Over Time repeat panel always shows single "All" panel
